  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The operative concept 'predictive skill' is defined as inter-model agreement (low spread), not accuracy against observations. This conflation is the root conceptual flaw; reconstructing the thesis requires interrogating whether agreement has been validly substituted for skill.",
    "R1.2": "Load-bearing: the headline conclusion ('high predictive skill across all six regions') is directly contradicted by the reported results (Northeast Brazil skill=0.41, sign disagreement; SE Australia skill=0.48, four models sign-reversed) and by the logical gap that model agreement does not entail forecast accuracy.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing: the headline and Discussion claim six-region coverage, but headline statistics silently exclude two of six regions. The stated rationale ('known structural uncertainty') is post-hoc; inclusion/exclusion rules were not pre-specified. Claim-evidence mismatch is severe."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I check the logical and scientific validity of the claims and methodology (e.g., whether the conclusions follow from the evidence), or the quality of the writing and prose (e.g., clarity, flow, grammar)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The operative concept 'predictive skill' is defined as inter-model agreement (low spread), not accuracy against observations. This conflation is the root conceptual flaw; reconstructing the thesis requires interrogating whether agreement has been validly substituted for skill.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the headline conclusion ('high predictive skill across all six regions') is directly contradicted by the reported results (Northeast Brazil skill=0.41, sign disagreement; SE Australia skill=0.48, four models sign-reversed) and by the logical gap that model agreement does not entail forecast accuracy.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the headline and Discussion claim six-region coverage, but headline statistics silently exclude two of six regions. The stated rationale ('known structural uncertainty') is post-hoc; inclusion/exclusion rules were not pre-specified. Claim-evidence mismatch is severe.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I check the logical and scientific validity of the claims and methodology (e.g., whether the conclusions follow from the evidence), or the quality of the writing and prose (e.g., clarity, flow, grammar)?
